Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is a city in Nigeria known for its economic development and infrastructure. It is a major hub for business and industry, with a growing population and diverse cultural influences. The city is home to several universities and research institutions, contributing to its reputation as a center of learning and innovation.  

Sec. 2  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is also a significant agricultural center, with fertile land and favorable climate conditions that support farming and livestock production. The region is known for its cotton and other agricultural products, which are vital to the national economy.  

Sec. 3  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki has a rich historical background, with roots in the early 20th century when it was established as a military and administrative center. Over time, it evolved into a modern city with a mix of traditional and contemporary architecture.  

Sec. 4  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
The city is well-connected by road and air, with major highways and airports that facilitate trade and travel. It is also a key transportation hub, linking Nigeria to other parts of the country and the world.  

Sec. 5  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is home to several important landmarks, including the Lekki International Airport, the Lekki Development Authority, and the Lekki High School. These institutions play a crucial role in the city's development and growth.  

Sec. 6  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki
